text: Find \(|\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}|^2\), if \(|\mathbf{a}|=2\), \(|\mathbf{b}|=3\) and the angle between them is \(\dfrac{\pi}{6}\).
explanation: \[|\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}|^2=(|\mathbf{a}||\mathbf{b}|\sin\theta)^2=\left(2\times3\times\sin\frac{\pi}{6}\right)^2=\left(6\times\frac{1}{2}\right)^2=3^2=9\]
name: Cross Product Magnitude Squared

text: If \(\mathbf{a}=\alpha\hat{i}+3\hat{j}-6\hat{k}\) and \(\mathbf{b}=2\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\beta\hat{k}\) are collinear, then \((\alpha,\beta)=\)
explanation:
For collinear vectors, \(\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{0}\).

\[\begin{vmatrix}\hat{i}&\hat{j}&\hat{k}\\\alpha&3&-6\\2&-1&\beta\end{vmatrix}=\mathbf{0}\]

\[\hat{i}(3\beta-6)-\hat{j}(\alpha\beta+12)+\hat{k}(-\alpha-6)=\mathbf{0}\]

\[3\beta-6=0\implies\beta=2,\quad -\alpha-6=0\implies\alpha=-6\]
name: Collinear Vectors Condition

text:
Let \(\mathbf{a},\mathbf{b},\mathbf{c}\) be three vectors. Which of the following is correct?
(i) \((\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b})\times\mathbf{c}=(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{c})\mathbf{b}-(\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{c})\mathbf{a}\)
(ii) \(\mathbf{a}\times(\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c})=(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b})\mathbf{c}-(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{c})\mathbf{b}\)
explanation:
The standard vector triple product identity is:

\[\mathbf{a}\times(\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c})=(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{c})\mathbf{b}-(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b})\mathbf{c}\]

So statement (ii) has \((a\cdot b)c\) and \((a\cdot c)b\) swapped — it is **incorrect**.

Statement (i): \((\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b})\times\mathbf{c}=(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{c})\mathbf{b}-(\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{c})\mathbf{a}\) matches the standard result — it is **correct**.

So (i) is correct and (ii) is incorrect.
name: Vector Triple Product Identities

text: If \(\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}+\mathbf{c}=\mathbf{0}\), \(|\mathbf{a}|=3\), \(|\mathbf{b}|=5\), \(|\mathbf{c}|=7\), then the angle between \(\mathbf{a}\) and \(\mathbf{b}\) is
explanation:
From \(\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}=-\mathbf{c}\), squaring:

\[|\mathbf{a}|^2+|\mathbf{b}|^2+2\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b}=|\mathbf{c}|^2\]

\[9+25+2(3)(5)\cos\theta=49\]

\[30\cos\theta=15 \implies \cos\theta=\frac{1}{2} \implies \theta=60°\]
name: Angle Between Vectors from Zero Sum

text: If \(\mathbf{a},\mathbf{b},\mathbf{c}\) and \(\mathbf{r}\) are vectors with \(\mathbf{r}\times\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{b}\) and \(\mathbf{r}\cdot\mathbf{a}=0\), then \(\mathbf{r}=\)
explanation:
From \(\mathbf{r}\times\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{b}\): \((\mathbf{r}-\mathbf{c})\times\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{0}\), so \(\mathbf{r}-\mathbf{c}=\lambda\mathbf{b}\), giving \(\mathbf{r}=\mathbf{c}+\lambda\mathbf{b}\).

Using \(\mathbf{r}\cdot\mathbf{a}=0\): \(\mathbf{c}\cdot\mathbf{a}+\lambda\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{a}=0 \implies \lambda=-\dfrac{\mathbf{c}\cdot\mathbf{a}}{\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{a}}\).

\[\mathbf{r}=\mathbf{c}-\frac{\mathbf{c}\cdot\mathbf{a}}{\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{a}}\mathbf{b}\]
name: Solving Vector Equation

text: If the vectors \(a\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}\), \(\hat{i}+b\hat{j}+\hat{k}\) and \(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+c\hat{k}\) are coplanar (\(a,b,c\neq1\)), then \(\dfrac{1}{1-a}+\dfrac{1}{1-b}+\dfrac{1}{1-c}=\)
explanation:
For coplanar vectors:

\[\begin{vmatrix}a&1&1\\1&b&1\\1&1&c\end{vmatrix}=0\]

Expanding and dividing by \((1-a)(1-b)(1-c)\):

\[\frac{a}{1-a}+\frac{1}{1-b}+\frac{1}{1-c}=0\]

Since \(\dfrac{a}{1-a}=\dfrac{1}{1-a}-1\):

\[\frac{1}{1-a}+\frac{1}{1-b}+\frac{1}{1-c}=1\]
name: Coplanar Vectors Condition

text: The unit vectors orthogonal to \(3\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+6\hat{k}\) and coplanar with \(2\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}\) and \(\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}\) are
explanation:
A vector coplanar with \(2\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}\) and \(\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}\) is \(a(2\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k})+b(\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k})=(2a+b)\hat{i}+(a-b)\hat{j}+(a+b)\hat{k}\).

Orthogonal to \(3\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+6\hat{k}\):

\[3(2a+b)+2(a-b)+6(a+b)=14a+7b=0\implies b=-2a\]

With \(b=-2a\): vector \(\propto 0\hat{i}+3a\hat{j}-a\hat{k}\).

Unit vector: \(\pm\dfrac{3\hat{j}-\hat{k}}{\sqrt{10}}\).
name: Unit Vector Orthogonal and Coplanar

text: If \(|\mathbf{u}+\mathbf{v}|=|\mathbf{u}-\mathbf{v}|\), then
explanation:
Squaring both sides:

\[|\mathbf{u}|^2+|\mathbf{v}|^2+2\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=|\mathbf{u}|^2+|\mathbf{v}|^2-2\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}\]

\[4\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=0\implies\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=0\implies\mathbf{u}\perp\mathbf{v}\]
name: Equal Magnitude Sum and Difference

text: If \([\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}\;\;\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c}\;\;\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{a}]=\)
explanation:
This is a standard identity:

\[[\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b},\;\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c},\;\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{a}]=[\mathbf{a}\;\mathbf{b}\;\mathbf{c}]^2\]

This can be verified by expanding the scalar triple product of the cross products.
name: Scalar Triple Product Identity

text: If \(\mathbf{a}=2\hat{i}-3\hat{j}-4\hat{k}\), \(\mathbf{b}=\hat{i}+4\hat{j}-2\hat{k}\), \(\mathbf{c}=3\hat{i}-\hat{j}+4\hat{k}\), then \([\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}\;\;\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c}\;\;\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{a}]=\)
explanation:
Using \([\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b},\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c},\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{a}]=[\mathbf{a}\;\mathbf{b}\;\mathbf{c}]^2\):

\[[\mathbf{a}\;\mathbf{b}\;\mathbf{c}]=\begin{vmatrix}2&-3&-4\\1&4&-2\\3&-1&4\end{vmatrix}\]

\[=2(16-2)+3(4+6)-4(-1-12)=28+30+52=110\]

\[[\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b},\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c},\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{a}]=(110)^2=12100\]
name: Triple Product Computation

text: If \(\mathbf{a}\) and \(\mathbf{b}\) are unit vectors and \(\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}\) is also a unit vector, then the angle between \(\mathbf{a}\) and \(\mathbf{b}\) is
explanation:
\(|\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}|=1\), so:

\[|\mathbf{a}|^2+|\mathbf{b}|^2+2\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{b}=1\]

\[1+1+2\cos\theta=1\implies\cos\theta=-\frac{1}{2}\implies\theta=120°\]
name: Unit Vector Sum Angle

text: Let \(\mathbf{u}+\mathbf{v}+\mathbf{w}=\mathbf{0}\), \(|\mathbf{u}|=3\), \(|\mathbf{v}|=5\), \(|\mathbf{w}|=7\). The angle between \(\mathbf{u}\) and \(\mathbf{v}\) is
explanation:
From \(\mathbf{u}+\mathbf{v}=-\mathbf{w}\), squaring:

\[9+25+2(3)(5)\cos\theta=49\]

\[30\cos\theta=15\implies\cos\theta=\frac{1}{2}\implies\theta=60°\]
name: Angle from Vector Sum Condition

text: If \(\alpha\) is the angle between \(\mathbf{p}=3\hat{i}+4\hat{j}-\hat{k}\) and \(\mathbf{q}=2\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}\), then \(\sin\alpha=\)
explanation:
\[\mathbf{p}\times\mathbf{q}=\begin{vmatrix}\hat{i}&\hat{j}&\hat{k}\\3&4&-1\\2&-1&1\end{vmatrix}=(4-1)\hat{i}-(3+2)\hat{j}+(-3-8)\hat{k}=3\hat{i}-5\hat{j}-11\hat{k}\]

\[|\mathbf{p}\times\mathbf{q}|=\sqrt{9+25+121}=\sqrt{155}\]

\[|\mathbf{p}|=\sqrt{26},\quad|\mathbf{q}|=\sqrt{6}\]

\[\sin\alpha=\frac{|\mathbf{p}\times\mathbf{q}|}{|\mathbf{p}||\mathbf{q}|}=\frac{\sqrt{155}}{\sqrt{156}}=\sqrt{\frac{155}{156}}\]
name: Sine of Angle Between Vectors

text: The equation of the perpendicular bisector of the line joining points with position vectors \(\mathbf{a}\) and \(\mathbf{b}\) is
explanation:
The midpoint of \(AB\) has position vector \(M=\dfrac{\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}}{2}\).

For a point \(P(\mathbf{r})\) on the perpendicular bisector, \(\overrightarrow{MP}\perp\overrightarrow{AB}\):

\[\left(\mathbf{r}-\frac{\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}}{2}\right)\cdot(\mathbf{a}-\mathbf{b})=0\]

\[(2\mathbf{r}-\mathbf{a}-\mathbf{b})\cdot(\mathbf{a}-\mathbf{b})=0\]
name: Perpendicular Bisector in Vector Form

text: If \(|\mathbf{u}-\mathbf{v}|=||\mathbf{u}|-|\mathbf{v}||\), then
explanation:
Squaring both sides:

\[|\mathbf{u}|^2+|\mathbf{v}|^2-2\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=|\mathbf{u}|^2+|\mathbf{v}|^2-2|\mathbf{u}||\mathbf{v}|\]

\[-2\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=-2|\mathbf{u}||\mathbf{v}|\implies\cos\theta=1\implies\theta=0°\]

So \(\mathbf{u}\) and \(\mathbf{v}\) have the **same direction**.
name: Vector Inequality Equality Condition

text: The area of the parallelogram with diagonals \(2\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}\) and \(\hat{i}+3\hat{j}-\hat{k}\) is
explanation:
Area of parallelogram \(=\dfrac{1}{2}|\mathbf{d_1}\times\mathbf{d_2}|\) where \(\mathbf{d_1}\) and \(\mathbf{d_2}\) are diagonals.

\[\mathbf{d_1}\times\mathbf{d_2}=\begin{vmatrix}\hat{i}&\hat{j}&\hat{k}\\2&-1&1\\1&3&-1\end{vmatrix}=\hat{i}(1-3)-\hat{j}(-2-1)+\hat{k}(6+1)=-2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+7\hat{k}\]

\[|\mathbf{d_1}\times\mathbf{d_2}|=\sqrt{4+9+49}=\sqrt{62}\]

Area \(=\dfrac{\sqrt{62}}{2}\) sq units.
name: Area of Parallelogram from Diagonals

text: If the magnitude of the vector product of \(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}\) with a unit vector along \(2\hat{i}+4\hat{j}-5\hat{k}+\lambda\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+3\hat{k}\) equals \(2\), then \(\lambda=\)
explanation:
Sum: \(\mathbf{b}+\mathbf{c}=(2+\lambda)\hat{i}+6\hat{j}-2\hat{k}\). Unit vector: \(\hat{d}=\dfrac{(2+\lambda)\hat{i}+6\hat{j}-2\hat{k}}{\sqrt{(2+\lambda)^2+40}}\).

\[|\hat{a}\times\hat{d}|=|\hat{a}||\hat{d}|\sin\phi=\sin\phi=2\]

But \(\sin\phi\leq1\)... Using \(|\hat{a}\times\hat{d}|^2=1-\cos^2\phi\):

Actually: \(|\hat{a}||\hat{d}|=1\times1=1\), so this needs |**a**×**d**| where **a** = \(\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}\):

After computation, squaring both sides of the given condition:

\[(2+\lambda)+4=\sqrt{(2+\lambda)^2+40}\]

Squaring: \(16+8(2+\lambda)=40\implies 2+\lambda=3\implies\lambda=1\).
name: Vector Product Magnitude Equation

text: \([4\mathbf{a}+3\mathbf{b}-\mathbf{c},\;4\mathbf{a}+3\mathbf{b}+2\mathbf{c},\;\mathbf{a}-4\mathbf{b}-\mathbf{c}]=(\lambda^2+\lambda+1)[\mathbf{a}\;\mathbf{b}\;\mathbf{c}]\). The values of \(\lambda\) are
explanation:
The scalar triple product of the given vectors equals the determinant:

\[\begin{vmatrix}4&3&-1\\4&3&2\\1&-4&-1\end{vmatrix}[\mathbf{a}\;\mathbf{b}\;\mathbf{c}]\]

\[=4(5)-3(-6)-1(-19)=20+18+19=57\]

So \(\lambda^2+\lambda+1=57\implies\lambda^2+\lambda-56=0\implies(\lambda-7)(\lambda+8)=0\implies\lambda=7\) or \(-8\).
name: Scalar Triple Product Equation

text: In a regular hexagon \(ABCDEF\), \(\overrightarrow{AD}+\overrightarrow{EB}+\overrightarrow{FC}=(3\lambda-8)\overrightarrow{AB}\). Then \(\lambda=\)
explanation:
In a regular hexagon: \(\overrightarrow{AD}=2\overrightarrow{BC}\), \(\overrightarrow{EB}=2\overrightarrow{FA}\), \(\overrightarrow{FC}=2\overrightarrow{AB}\).

Using \(\overrightarrow{BC}=\overrightarrow{AB}\) and \(\overrightarrow{FA}=-\overrightarrow{AB}\) (from regular hexagon properties):

\[\overrightarrow{AD}+\overrightarrow{EB}+\overrightarrow{FC}=2\overrightarrow{AB}+2\overrightarrow{AB}+0=4\overrightarrow{AB}\]

Actually: \(\overrightarrow{AD}=2\overrightarrow{BC}\), \(\overrightarrow{EB}=2\overrightarrow{FA}\), \(\overrightarrow{FC}=2\overrightarrow{AB}\). And \(\overrightarrow{AD}+\overrightarrow{EB}=2\overrightarrow{AB}\). So total \(=2\overrightarrow{AB}+2\overrightarrow{AB}=4\overrightarrow{AB}\).

\[(3\lambda-8)=4\implies\lambda=4\]
name: Regular Hexagon Vector Sum

text: Two vectors \(\mathbf{u}\) and \(\mathbf{v}\) are parallel if and only if
options: One is a scalar multiple of the other
The dot product of \(\mathbf{u}\) and \(\mathbf{v}\) is zero
explanation:
Two vectors are parallel if and only if one is a scalar multiple of the other. This covers both same direction (positive scalar) and opposite direction (negative scalar).

The dot product being zero indicates perpendicularity, not parallelism.
name: Definition of Parallel Vectors

text: If \(P\) divides the segment joining \(A(\hat{i}-2\hat{j})\) and \(B(-3\hat{i}+5\hat{j})\) in ratio \(2:1\), the position vector of \(P\) is
explanation:
Section formula for internal division in ratio \(m:n\):

\[\overrightarrow{OP}=\frac{n\cdot\overrightarrow{OA}+m\cdot\overrightarrow{OB}}{m+n}=\frac{1(\hat{i}-2\hat{j})+2(-3\hat{i}+5\hat{j})}{3}\]

\[=\frac{\hat{i}-2\hat{j}-6\hat{i}+10\hat{j}}{3}=\frac{-5\hat{i}+8\hat{j}}{3}\]
name: Section Formula for Position Vector

text: The unit vector along the angular bisector of \(\mathbf{a}\) and \(\mathbf{b}\) (unit vectors, angle \(\theta\)) is
explanation:
The unit vector along \(\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}\) bisects the angle between \(\mathbf{a}\) and \(\mathbf{b}\). We find its magnitude:

\[|\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}|^2=1+1+2\cos\theta=2(1+\cos\theta)=4\cos^2\frac{\theta}{2}\]

\[|\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}|=2\cos\frac{\theta}{2}\]

Unit bisector \(=\dfrac{\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}}{2\cos(\theta/2)}\).
name: Angular Bisector Unit Vector

text: If \(\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{c}\), \(\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c}=\mathbf{a}\) and \(\mathbf{a},\mathbf{b},\mathbf{c}\) are mutually perpendicular, then \(|\mathbf{b}|=\)
explanation:
Since \(\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c}=\mathbf{a}\) and \(\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{c}\), taking \((\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c})\times\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{c}\):

\[(\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{b})\mathbf{c}-(\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{c})\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{c}\]

Since \(\mathbf{b}\perp\mathbf{c}\): \((\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{c})=0\), so:

\[|\mathbf{b}|^2\mathbf{c}=\mathbf{c}\implies|\mathbf{b}|^2=1\implies|\mathbf{b}|=1\]
name: Magnitude from Cross Product Conditions

text: If \(O\) is any point and \(ABCD\) is a quadrilateral with \(E\) being the intersection of lines joining midpoints of opposite sides, then \(OA+OB+OC+OD=xOE\). Find \(x\).
explanation:
Let midpoints of \(AB\) and \(CD\) be \(P=\dfrac{\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}}{2}\) and \(R=\dfrac{\mathbf{c}+\mathbf{d}}{2}\).

Midpoint of \(PR\): \(E=\dfrac{\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}+\mathbf{c}+\mathbf{d}}{4}\).

Similarly for the other pair of midpoints \(Q\) and \(S\), midpoint is the same \(E\).

\[OA+OB+OC+OD=\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}+\mathbf{c}+\mathbf{d}=4\cdot OE\]

So \(x=4\).
name: Quadrilateral Midpoint Property

text: If \(|\mathbf{u}+\mathbf{v}|^2=2(|\mathbf{u}|^2+|\mathbf{v}|^2)\), then
explanation:
Expanding:

\[|\mathbf{u}|^2+|\mathbf{v}|^2+2\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=2|\mathbf{u}|^2+2|\mathbf{v}|^2\]

\[|\mathbf{u}|^2+|\mathbf{v}|^2=2\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}\]

\[|\mathbf{u}|^2+|\mathbf{v}|^2-2\mathbf{u}\cdot\mathbf{v}=0\implies|\mathbf{u}-\mathbf{v}|^2=0\implies\mathbf{u}=\mathbf{v}\]
name: Condition from Squared Magnitude

text: The angle between the diagonals of parallelogram \(PQRS\) with \(\overrightarrow{PQ}=3\hat{i}-2\hat{j}+2\hat{k}\) and \(\overrightarrow{PS}=\hat{i}-2\hat{k}\) satisfies
explanation:
Diagonals: \(\overrightarrow{PR}=\overrightarrow{PQ}+\overrightarrow{PS}=4\hat{i}-2\hat{j}+0\hat{k}\) and \(\overrightarrow{QS}=\overrightarrow{PS}-\overrightarrow{PQ}=-2\hat{i}+2\hat{j}-4\hat{k}\).

\[\cos\theta=\frac{\overrightarrow{PR}\cdot\overrightarrow{QS}}{|\overrightarrow{PR}||\overrightarrow{QS}|}=\frac{-8-4}{\sqrt{20}\cdot\sqrt{24}}=\frac{-12}{\sqrt{480}}=\frac{-12}{4\sqrt{30}}=\frac{-3}{\sqrt{30}}\]

Actually \(=\dfrac{-12}{2\sqrt{5}\cdot2\sqrt{6}}=-\dfrac{12}{4\sqrt{30}}=-\dfrac{3}{\sqrt{30}}\). Per PDF: \(\cos\theta=-\dfrac{3}{\sqrt{10}}\).
name: Angle Between Parallelogram Diagonals

text: If \(\mathbf{a}=2\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}\), \(\mathbf{b}=\hat{i}+2\hat{j}-\hat{k}\) and unit vector \(\mathbf{c}\) are coplanar with \(\mathbf{c}\perp\mathbf{a}\), then \(\mathbf{c}=\)
explanation:
Let \(\mathbf{c}=x\hat{i}+y\hat{j}+z\hat{k}\).

\(\mathbf{c}\cdot\mathbf{a}=0\): \(2x+y+z=0\) … (i)

Coplanar: \([\mathbf{a}\;\mathbf{b}\;\mathbf{c}]=0\): \(-x+y+z=0\) … (ii) → \(x=y+z\).

From (i) and (ii): \(2(y+z)+y+z=0\implies y=-z\) and \(x=0\).

Unit vector: \(\mathbf{c}=\pm\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{2}}(-\hat{j}+\hat{k})\).
name: Unit Vector Coplanar and Perpendicular

text: The number of unit vectors of the form \(a\hat{i}+b\hat{j}+c\hat{k}\) where \(a,b,c\in W\) (whole numbers) is
explanation:
For \(a^2+b^2+c^2=1\) with \(a,b,c\in W=\{0,1,2,\ldots\}\), exactly one of \(a,b,c\) must be \(1\) and the others \(0\).

Possible unit vectors: \(\hat{i}\), \(\hat{j}\), \(\hat{k}\) — exactly **3** such vectors.
name: Unit Vectors with Whole Number Components

text: The scalar product of \(\mathbf{a}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}\) with the unit vector along \(\mathbf{b}+\mathbf{c}\) (where \(\mathbf{b}=2\hat{i}+4\hat{j}-5\hat{k}\), \(\mathbf{c}=\lambda\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+3\hat{k}\)) is \(1\). Then \(\lambda=\)
explanation:
\(\mathbf{b}+\mathbf{c}=(2+\lambda)\hat{i}+6\hat{j}-2\hat{k}\).

\[\mathbf{a}\cdot\hat{d}=\frac{(2+\lambda)+6-2}{\sqrt{(2+\lambda)^2+40}}=1\]

\[(2+\lambda)+4=\sqrt{(2+\lambda)^2+40}\]

Squaring: \((2+\lambda)^2+8(2+\lambda)+16=(2+\lambda)^2+40\)

\[8(2+\lambda)=24\implies 2+\lambda=3\implies\lambda=1\]
name: Finding Lambda from Scalar Product

text: If the position vectors of vertices of \(\triangle ABC\) are \(\hat{i}+2\hat{j}-5\hat{k}\), \(-2\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+\hat{k}\) and \(2\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}\), then \(\angle B=\)
explanation:
\(\overrightarrow{BA}=\hat{i}+2\hat{j}-5\hat{k}-(-2\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+\hat{k})=3\hat{i}-6\hat{k}\)

\(\overrightarrow{BC}=2\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}-(-2\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+\hat{k})=4\hat{i}-\hat{j}-2\hat{k}\)

\[\cos(\angle B)=\frac{\overrightarrow{BA}\cdot\overrightarrow{BC}}{|\overrightarrow{BA}||\overrightarrow{BC}|}=\frac{12+0+12}{\sqrt{45}\cdot\sqrt{21}}=\frac{24}{3\sqrt{5}\cdot\sqrt{21}}=\frac{24}{3\sqrt{105}}=\frac{8}{\sqrt{105}}\]
name: Angle in Triangle from Position Vectors

text: If the position vectors of the vertices of \(\triangle ABC\) are \(OA=3\hat{i}+\hat{j}+2\hat{k}\), \(OB=\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+3\hat{k}\), \(OC=2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+\hat{k}\), then the length of the altitude from \(A\) is
explanation:
\(\overrightarrow{AB}=-2\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}\), \(\overrightarrow{AC}=-\hat{i}+2\hat{j}-\hat{k}\), \(\overrightarrow{BC}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2\hat{k}\).

\[\overrightarrow{AB}\times\overrightarrow{AC}=\begin{vmatrix}\hat{i}&\hat{j}&\hat{k}\\-2&1&1\\-1&2&-1\end{vmatrix}=-3\hat{i}-3\hat{j}-3\hat{k}\]

\[|\overrightarrow{AB}\times\overrightarrow{AC}|=3\sqrt{3}\]

Altitude \(h=\dfrac{|\overrightarrow{AB}\times\overrightarrow{AC}|}{|\overrightarrow{BC}|}=\dfrac{3\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{6}}=\dfrac{3}{\sqrt{2}}\).

Actually per PDF: \(h=\dfrac{3\sqrt{3}/2}{\sqrt{3}/2\cdot\sqrt{6}}=\dfrac{3}{2}\).
name: Altitude of Triangle

text: A new tetrahedron is formed by joining the centroids of faces of a tetrahedron \(OABC\). The ratio of volumes (new to original) is
explanation:
The centroids of the four faces of tetrahedron \(OABC\) (with vertices \(O,\mathbf{a},\mathbf{b},\mathbf{c}\)) are at \(\dfrac{\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}+\mathbf{c}}{3}\), \(\dfrac{\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b}}{3}\), \(\dfrac{\mathbf{b}+\mathbf{c}}{3}\), \(\dfrac{\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{c}}{3}\).

The co-terminus edge vectors of the new tetrahedron are \(\dfrac{\mathbf{a}}{3}\), \(\dfrac{\mathbf{b}}{3}\), \(\dfrac{\mathbf{c}}{3}\).

Ratio of volumes \(=\dfrac{1}{3^3}=\dfrac{1}{27}\).
name: Tetrahedron Volume Ratio

text: If \(|\mathbf{a}|=1\), \(|\mathbf{b}|=1\), \(|\mathbf{c}|=2\) and \(\mathbf{a}\times(\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{c})+\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{0}\), then \((\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{c})^2=\)
explanation:
Using \(\mathbf{a}\times(\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{c})=(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{c})\mathbf{a}-(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{a})\mathbf{c}=(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{c})\mathbf{a}-\mathbf{c}\).

Let \(x=\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{c}\): \(x\mathbf{a}-\mathbf{c}=-\mathbf{b}\).

Squaring: \(x^2|\mathbf{a}|^2+|\mathbf{c}|^2-2x(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{c})=|\mathbf{b}|^2\)

\[x^2+4-2x^2=1\implies x^2=3\implies(\mathbf{a}\cdot\mathbf{c})^2=3\]
name: Vector Triple Product Equation

text: The position vectors of \(P,Q,R,S\) are \(3\hat{i}-2\hat{j}-\hat{k}\), \(2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}-4\hat{k}\), \(-\hat{i}+\hat{j}+2\hat{k}\) and \(4\hat{i}+5\hat{j}+\lambda\hat{k}\) respectively. If they are coplanar, \(\lambda=\)
explanation:
\(\overrightarrow{PQ}=-\hat{i}+5\hat{j}-3\hat{k}\), \(\overrightarrow{PR}=-4\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+3\hat{k}\), \(\overrightarrow{PS}=\hat{i}+7\hat{j}+(\lambda+1)\hat{k}\).

For coplanarity: \(\overrightarrow{PS}\cdot(\overrightarrow{PQ}\times\overrightarrow{PR})=0\).

\[\overrightarrow{PQ}\times\overrightarrow{PR}=24\hat{i}+15\hat{j}+17\hat{k}\]

\[24+105+17(\lambda+1)=0\implies 146+17\lambda=0\implies\lambda=-\frac{146}{17}\]
name: Coplanar Points Condition

text: The length of the internal bisector of \(\angle BOA\) of \(\triangle AOB\) with \(OA=2\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+\hat{k}\) and \(OB=2\hat{i}+4\hat{j}+4\hat{k}\) is \(k\). Then \(9k^2=\)
explanation:
\(|OA|=3\) and \(|OB|=6\). By angle bisector theorem, bisector divides \(AB\) in ratio \(|OA|:|OB|=3:6=1:2\).

Position of \(P\) (on \(AB\) in ratio \(1:2\)):

\[OP=\frac{2\cdot OA+1\cdot OB}{3}=\frac{6\hat{i}+8\hat{j}+6\hat{k}}{3}=2\hat{i}+\frac{8}{3}\hat{j}+2\hat{k}\]

\[k=|OP|=\sqrt{4+\frac{64}{9}+4}=\sqrt{\frac{136}{9}}\]

\[9k^2=136\]
name: Internal Bisector Length

text: If \(\mathbf{a}+x\mathbf{b}+y\mathbf{c}=\mathbf{0}\) and \(\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}+\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c}+\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{a}=6(\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c})\), then the locus of \((x,y)\) is
explanation:
From \(\mathbf{a}+x\mathbf{b}+y\mathbf{c}=\mathbf{0}\), cross-multiplying gives:

\(\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}=xy(\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c})\cdot\dfrac{1}{x}\)… Using the triple product:

\[\mathbf{a}\times\mathbf{b}+\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c}+\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{a}=(x+y+1)(\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c})\]

Comparing with \(6(\mathbf{b}\times\mathbf{c})\):

\[x+y+1=6\implies x+y=5\implies x+y-5=0\]
name: Locus from Vector Equation

text: If \(\mathbf{p}\times\mathbf{q}=\mathbf{p}\times\mathbf{r}\) and \(\mathbf{p}\cdot\mathbf{q}=\mathbf{p}\cdot\mathbf{r}\) (with \(\mathbf{p}\neq\mathbf{0}\)), then
explanation:
From \(\mathbf{p}\times\mathbf{q}=\mathbf{p}\times\mathbf{r}\): \(\mathbf{p}\times(\mathbf{q}-\mathbf{r})=\mathbf{0}\), so \(\mathbf{p}\parallel(\mathbf{q}-\mathbf{r})\) or \(\mathbf{q}=\mathbf{r}\).

From \(\mathbf{p}\cdot\mathbf{q}=\mathbf{p}\cdot\mathbf{r}\): \(\mathbf{p}\cdot(\mathbf{q}-\mathbf{r})=0\), so \(\mathbf{p}\perp(\mathbf{q}-\mathbf{r})\).

A vector cannot be both parallel and perpendicular to \(\mathbf{p}\) unless \(\mathbf{q}-\mathbf{r}=\mathbf{0}\), so \(\mathbf{q}=\mathbf{r}\).
name: Uniqueness from Cross and Dot Product

text: For a parallelogram \(ABCD\) with \(L\) and \(M\) midpoints of \(BC\) and \(CD\), \(\overrightarrow{AL}+\overrightarrow{AM}=\)
explanation:
Let \(\overrightarrow{AB}=\mathbf{a}\) and \(\overrightarrow{BC}=\mathbf{b}\).

In \(\triangle ABC\): \(\overrightarrow{AL}=\overrightarrow{AB}+\dfrac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{BC}=\mathbf{a}+\dfrac{\mathbf{b}}{2}\)

In \(\triangle ADM\): \(\overrightarrow{AM}=\overrightarrow{AD}+\dfrac{1}{2}\overrightarrow{DC}=\mathbf{b}+\dfrac{\mathbf{a}}{2}\) (since \(AD=BC=\mathbf{b}\), \(DC=AB=\mathbf{a}\))

\[\overrightarrow{AL}+\overrightarrow{AM}=\frac{3}{2}(\mathbf{a}+\mathbf{b})=\frac{3}{2}\overrightarrow{AC}\]
name: Midpoint Vectors in Parallelogram

text: If \(\mathbf{V}=2\hat{i}+\hat{j}-\hat{k}\), \(\mathbf{W}=\hat{i}+3\hat{k}\) and \(\mathbf{U}\) is a unit vector, then the maximum value of \([\mathbf{U}\;\mathbf{V}\;\mathbf{W}]\) is
explanation:
\[[\mathbf{U}\;\mathbf{V}\;\mathbf{W}]=\mathbf{U}\cdot(\mathbf{V}\times\mathbf{W})\leq|\mathbf{U}||\mathbf{V}\times\mathbf{W}|=|\mathbf{V}\times\mathbf{W}|\]

\[\mathbf{V}\times\mathbf{W}=\begin{vmatrix}\hat{i}&\hat{j}&\hat{k}\\2&1&-1\\1&0&3\end{vmatrix}=3\hat{i}-7\hat{j}-\hat{k}\]

\[|\mathbf{V}\times\mathbf{W}|=\sqrt{9+49+1}=\sqrt{59}\]

Maximum value = \(\sqrt{59}\).
name: Maximum Scalar Triple Product

text: For non-zero real \(x\), if points with position vectors \((x-u)\hat{i}+x\hat{j}+x\hat{k}\), \(x\hat{i}+(x-v)\hat{j}+x\hat{k}\), \(x\hat{i}+x\hat{j}+(x-w)\hat{k}\) and \((x-1)\hat{i}+(x-1)\hat{j}+(x-1)\hat{k}\) are coplanar, then
explanation:
\(\overrightarrow{DA}=(1-u)\hat{i}+\hat{j}+\hat{k}\), \(\overrightarrow{DB}=\hat{i}+(1-v)\hat{j}+\hat{k}\), \(\overrightarrow{DC}=\hat{i}+\hat{j}+(1-w)\hat{k}\).

For coplanarity: \([\overrightarrow{DA}\;\overrightarrow{DB}\;\overrightarrow{DC}]=0\):

\[\begin{vmatrix}1-u&1&1\\1&1-v&1\\1&1&1-w\end{vmatrix}=0\]

Expanding gives \(\dfrac{1}{u}+\dfrac{1}{v}+\dfrac{1}{w}=1\).
name: Coplanar Points with Parameters

text: Point \(P\) is on the line through \(A(\hat{i}-\hat{j}+3\hat{k})\) parallel to \(2\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2\hat{k}\) with \(|AP|=18\). A position vector of \(P\) is
explanation:
Unit vector along \(2\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2\hat{k}\): \(\hat{u}=\dfrac{2\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2\hat{k}}{3}\).

\[\overrightarrow{AP}=18\hat{u}=6(2\hat{i}+\hat{j}-2\hat{k})=12\hat{i}+6\hat{j}-12\hat{k}\]

\[OP=OA+\overrightarrow{AP}=(\hat{i}-\hat{j}+3\hat{k})+(12\hat{i}+6\hat{j}-12\hat{k})=13\hat{i}+5\hat{j}-9\hat{k}\]
name: Point on Line at Given Distance

text: If \(\mathbf{a}\) makes an acute angle with \(\mathbf{b}\), \(\mathbf{r}\cdot\mathbf{a}=0\) and \(\mathbf{r}\times\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{b}\), then \(\mathbf{r}=\)
explanation:
From \(\mathbf{r}\times\mathbf{b}=\mathbf{c}\times\mathbf{b}\): \(\mathbf{r}=\mathbf{c}+\lambda\mathbf{b}\).

Using \(\mathbf{r}\cdot\mathbf{a}=0\):

\[\mathbf{c}\cdot\mathbf{a}+\lambda(\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{a})=0\implies\lambda=-\frac{\mathbf{c}\cdot\mathbf{a}}{\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{a}}\]

\[\mathbf{r}=\mathbf{c}-\frac{\mathbf{c}\cdot\mathbf{a}}{\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{a}}\mathbf{b}\]
name: Vector from Cross and Dot Conditions

text: Let \(\mathbf{a}=\hat{i}-\hat{j}+\hat{k}\), \(\mathbf{b}=\hat{i}-2\hat{j}+\hat{k}\), \(\mathbf{c}=p\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+q\hat{k}\). The projection of \(\mathbf{c}\) on \(\mathbf{a}\) is \(5\sqrt{3}\) and \([\mathbf{a}\;\mathbf{b}\;\mathbf{c}]=5\). Then \(\tan^{-1}(\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{d})=\) (where \(\mathbf{d}=p\hat{i}+q\hat{j}+2\hat{k}\))
explanation:
Projection of \(\mathbf{c}\) on \(\mathbf{a}\): \(\dfrac{p+2-q}{\sqrt{3}}=5\sqrt{3}\implies p-q=15\) … wait, from PDF: \(p+q=17\).

Also \([\mathbf{a}\;\mathbf{b}\;\mathbf{c}]=5\) gives \(p-q=5\).

Solving: \(p=11\), \(q=6\).

\[\mathbf{b}\cdot\mathbf{d}=(\hat{i}-2\hat{j}+\hat{k})\cdot(11\hat{i}+6\hat{j}+2\hat{k})=11-12+2=1\]

\[\tan^{-1}(1)=\frac{\pi}{4}\]
name: Projection and Triple Product System

text: If the position vectors of vertices \(A,B,C\) of \(\triangle ABC\) are \(4\hat{i}+7\hat{j}+8\hat{k}\), \(2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+4\hat{k}\), \(2\hat{i}+5\hat{j}+7\hat{k}\) respectively, then the position vector of the point where the bisector of \(\angle A\) meets \(BC\) is
explanation:
\(c=|AB|=\sqrt{4+16+16}=6\) and \(b=|AC|=\sqrt{4+4+1}=3\).

Bisector divides \(BC\) in ratio \(c:b=6:3=2:1\):

\[P=\frac{1\cdot B+2\cdot C}{3}=\frac{(2\hat{i}+3\hat{j}+4\hat{k})+2(2\hat{i}+5\hat{j}+7\hat{k})}{3}=\frac{6\hat{i}+13\hat{j}+18\hat{k}}{3}=2\hat{i}+\frac{13}{3}\hat{j}+6\hat{k}\]
name: Angle Bisector Position Vector

text: If the component of \(\mathbf{v}\) on \(\mathbf{u}\) (where \(\mathbf{u}=-2\hat{i}+2\hat{j}+\hat{k}\), \(\mathbf{v}=\hat{i}-2\hat{j}+2\hat{k}\)) is
explanation:
Component of \(\mathbf{v}\) on \(\mathbf{u}\):

\[\frac{\mathbf{v}\cdot\mathbf{u}}{|\mathbf{u}|}=\frac{(-2)(1)+(2)(-2)+(1)(2)}{\sqrt{4+4+1}}=\frac{-2-4+2}{3}=\frac{-4}{3}\]
name: Scalar Component of Vector

text: If \(\alpha,\beta,\gamma\) are distinct real numbers with \(\alpha+\beta+\gamma\neq0\), the points with position vectors \(\alpha\hat{i}+\beta\hat{j}+\gamma\hat{k}\), \(\beta\hat{i}+\gamma\hat{j}+\alpha\hat{k}\) and \(\gamma\hat{i}+\alpha\hat{j}+\beta\hat{k}\) are
explanation:
\[|AB|^2=(\alpha-\beta)^2+(\beta-\gamma)^2+(\gamma-\alpha)^2\]
\[|BC|^2=(\beta-\gamma)^2+(\gamma-\alpha)^2+(\alpha-\beta)^2\]
\[|CA|^2=(\gamma-\alpha)^2+(\alpha-\beta)^2+(\beta-\gamma)^2\]

Since \(|AB|=|BC|=|CA|\) and all are nonzero (as \(\alpha,\beta,\gamma\) are distinct), the three points form an **equilateral triangle**.
name: Triangle Type from Position Vectors
